mnemonics.title: Percept Codes
mnemonics.content: S.B.G.B.S. = Stench (Wumpus nearby), Breeze (Pit nearby), Glitter (Gold here), Bump (Wall collision), Scream (Wumpus killed)

mnemonics.title: Action Sequence
mnemonics.content: M.T.G.S.C. = Move, Turn, Grab gold, Shoot arrow, Climb out - Remember 'My Tiger Grabs Sweet Carrots'

mnemonics.title: Grid Navigation
mnemonics.content: C.E.P.E. = Check percepts, Eliminate dangers, Plan path, Execute moves - 'Check Every Path Efficiently'

mnemonics.title: Reasoning Cycle
mnemonics.content: P.U.D.A. = Perceive, Update knowledge base, Decide action, Act - 'People Usually Dance After' perceiving dangers

acronyms.usage: Core elements to track in the environment
Agent's priority sequence: Avoid pits → Kill Wumpus → Get gold
Remember logical connectives for environment representation (AND for simultaneous percepts, OR for possible dangers)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a chessboard where dark squares = potential dangers, light squares = safe zones
Visualize Stench as red smoke clouds, Breeze as blue swirls, Glitter as yellow sparkles
Picture a growing tree where branches = possible safe paths, dead leaves = eliminated dangers
Think of safe movement as connecting green dots while avoiding red zones (like a circuit board)

analogies.analogy: Like detecting a sleeping bear in a campsite - smell (stench) warns you before seeing it
Similar to avoiding quicksand patches - test surrounding areas carefully after feeling breeze
Like retrieving a magnet from a minefield - plan exact retrieval path after location confirmation
Works like a detective's elimination board - cross out impossibilities to reveal safe path
